rationale: Assessment and first strength session already completed today — full rest to absorb the stimulus.

warmup: 5 min: leg swings (front/back and lateral, 10 each), hip circles, bodyweight squats × 10; then squat ramp: empty bar × 8, 40 kg × 5, 55 kg × 3, 65 kg × 1
cooldown: 5 min: standing quad stretch, kneeling hip flexor stretch, seated hamstring stretch, slow diaphragmatic breathing
rationale: First structured lower session of the program — sets the baseline for linear squat progression and builds posterior-chain volume with safe hip-hinge variations.

warmup: 5 min general: arm circles × 10 each direction, band pull-aparts × 15, shoulder CARs × 5 each side, light dumbbell lateral raise × 12 each side; then bench ramp: empty bar × 8, 35 kg × 5, 47.5 kg × 3
cooldown: 5 min: doorway pec stretch 30 s each side (gentle, no forcing), cross-body shoulder stretch 30 s each side, thoracic extension over bench edge 60 s, slow diaphragmatic breathing

exercises.name: Face Pulls (cable or band)
exercises.notes: Shoulder prehab — non-negotiable opener every upper session. Pull to forehead level, elbows high, controlled return.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Working weight 55 kg — first structured session so match baseline. Elbows 45–60° from torso, blades retracted and depressed. Stop immediately at any anterior shoulder discomfort.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Row (single-arm)
exercises.notes: Brace knee and hand on bench; drive elbow to hip, full retraction at top. Choose a weight where rep 10 is challenging but form stays tight.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Neutral or shoulder-width overhand grip; slight lean back, pull to upper chest, controlled 3-second eccentric. Avoid behind-the-neck position.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Light load; cable from low pulley, raise to shoulder height only — stop before impingement zone. Prefer cable over heavy dumbbell for rotator cuff safety.

rationale: Upper body session is unaffected by yesterday's walking fatigue and is kept as planned, with a mildly conservative approach given slightly elevated resting HR and suppressed HRV today.

warmup: 3 min easy water acclimation: 2 slow lengths any stroke, focus on breathing rhythm and loosening shoulders
cooldown: 3 min: 2 very easy backstroke lengths, then poolside calf stretch 30 s each leg, hip flexor lunge stretch 30 s each side, gentle shoulder cross-body stretch

exercises.name: Continuous Freestyle Swim
exercises.notes: Target avg HR 112–131 bpm (Zone 2). Aim to match or slightly exceed your 550 m baseline — target ~575–600 m if the effort feels comfortable. Pace should feel conversational; if HR climbs above 135 bpm, ease off. This is aerobic base work — no spikes. Log distance and avg HR via Garmin.

rationale: Zone 2 swim as originally planned — low mechanical load complements yesterday's upper strength session and gives legs further recovery after Sunday's heavy walking.
Scheduled rest day — maintains the planned recovery buffer before Thursday's lower body session.

warmup: 5 min general: leg swings front-back and lateral × 10 each, hip circles × 8 each direction, bodyweight squats × 10, glute bridges × 12; then squat ramp: empty bar × 8, 40 kg × 5, 57.5 kg × 3, 65 kg × 2
cooldown: 5 min: standing quad stretch 30 s each, kneeling hip flexor stretch 40 s each side, seated hamstring stretch 30 s each side, pigeon pose or figure-4 glute stretch 40 s each side

exercises.name: Barbell Back Squat
exercises.notes: Working weight 72.5 kg — this is your Week 1 starting point per the linear progression plan. Full depth, flat heels, braced core. If all 3 sets feel strong and controlled (RPE ≤ 8), you're on track to add 5 kg next session.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hip hinge accessory — NOT a conventional deadlift. Start at ~60–65 kg; hinge at hips, soft knee bend, bar stays close to legs, lower until you feel hamstring tension (typically mid-shin level). No rounding.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.notes: Use a dumbbell or kettlebell (~20–24 kg). Reinforces squat pattern, adds quad volume, low axial load. Pause 1 second at the bottom.

exercises.name: Leg Curl (machine)
exercises.notes: Hamstring isolation. Choose a weight where reps 10–12 are genuinely challenging. Controlled eccentric — 2–3 seconds lowering.

exercises.name: Plank Hold
exercises.notes: Forearm plank, neutral spine — no sagging hips. This is your baseline standard (90 s was your test result post-fatigue); aim for 60–75 s fresh with perfect form. Stop if hips drop.

rationale: Consolidates the missed Monday lower session into Thursday's planned slot — fresh after a full rest day, with the squat at the Week 1 baseline weight and posterior-chain accessories kept as hip-hinge variations to comply with the squat+deadlift separation rule.
Scheduled rest day to close out the week and allow full recovery before next week's training block begins.
